title: Evenen In The Village
content:
Now the light oï¿½ the west is a-turnï¿½d to gloom,
Anï¿½ the men be at hwome vrom ground;
Anï¿½ the bells be a-zendï¿½n all down the Coombe
From tower, their mwoansome sound.
Anï¿½ the wind is still,
Anï¿½ the house-dogs do bark,
Anï¿½ the rooks be a-vled to the elems high anï¿½ dark,
Anï¿½ the water do roar at mill.

Anï¿½ the flickerï¿½n light drough the window-peï¿½ne
Vrom the candleï¿½s dull fleï¿½me do shoot,
Anï¿½ young Jemmy the smith is a-gone down leï¿½ne,
A-plaï¿½ï¿½n his shrill-vaiced flute.
Anï¿½ the millerï¿½s man,
Do zit down at his ease
On the seat that is under the cluster oï¿½ trees,
Wiï¿½ his pipe anï¿½ his cider can.
meaning:
About: William Barnes (22nd February 1801 - 7th October 1886) was an English writer, poet, minister, and philologist. 
He wrote over 800 poems, some in Dorset dialect and much other work including a comprehensive English grammar quoting from more than 70 different languages.
